compiled_state.title: [merge-to-main]: local main diverges from origin/main -> merge phase conflicts requiring manual reset
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

**Component**: merge-to-main.sh `_phase_sync`
**Condition**: local main has commits not on origin/main (stale squash commits from a prior worktree session)
**Observed**: merge phase fails with textual conflict in plugin.json; `_squash_rebase_recovery` also fails; user must manually run `git reset --hard origin/main` on the main repo to unblock

## What Happened

During a worktree session on `worktree-20260417-164406`, `merge-to-main.sh` was run. The `_phase_sync` step fetched origin/main but did NOT reset local main to match it. Local main was at `7f54cd4c` (2 squash commits ahead of origin/main at `17b0b480`, created by a prior worktree session). When `_phase_merge` ran `git merge --no-ff $BRANCH` into local main, git saw both local main and the branch as having modified plugin.json relative to their common ancestor (`17b0b480`), causing a textual conflict — even though our branch's plugin.json was identical to origin/main.

`_squash_rebase_recovery` was attempted but also failed (`ERROR: git commit failed during squash — restoring HEAD`), likely because the pre-commit test gate rejected the squash commit (its diff hash didn't match any recorded test result).

## Root Cause

`_phase_sync` runs `git merge origin/main` into local main rather than `git reset --hard origin/main`. When local main has diverged (commits not on origin), the merge either fast-forwards (when local is behind) or creates a new merge commit — it does NOT handle the case where local main has commits that origin does NOT have. In that case, local main silently stays diverged, and `_phase_merge` later creates a false conflict.

## User Resolution Required

User ran:
```bash
git -C /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra reset --hard origin/main
```
Then deleted the state file and retried merge-to-main.

## Expected Behavior

`_phase_sync` should detect when local main has commits not on origin/main and either:
(a) hard-reset local main to origin/main after fetching (safe — local main should always mirror origin/main in this workflow), or
(b) warn the user with actionable instructions and abort rather than proceeding to a merge that will conflict

## Proposed Fix

In `_phase_sync`, after fetching, check whether local main is strictly ahead of origin/main:
```bash
ahead=$(git rev-list --count origin/main..main)
if [ "$ahead" -gt 0 ]; then
    git reset --hard origin/main  # or warn + abort
fi
```
